title: Under Kitchen Sink Organizer Shelf
summary: A simple two-board shelf sized to slide into the odd, plumbing-crowded space under a kitchen sink, with either a pass-through back or a full plywood back panel.
description:
This is a measure-first build with no fixed cut list — every dimension comes from your own cabinet opening, not a number on this page. Cut the overall height about 1" shorter than the cabinet opening so the organizer can actually be lifted in and out around the plumbing, and cut the shelf board 2" narrower than your desired final width, which leaves about 1/2" of side clearance for an easy fit.

Choose your back style based on what you're hiding: a pass-through design (an open lower section with just a 1x8 board partway up the back) works when you want to reach behind the shelf, while covering the full back with 1/4" plywood gives a cleaner look when there's nothing back there you need access to.

materials.name: 1x board, in your desired depth (1x8, 1x10, or 1x12)
1x8 board or 1/4" plywood, for the back
materials.note: 1x8 for a pass-through back, plywood to cover the full back.

materials.name: 1-1/4" brad nails or 1-1/2" SPAX self-tapping screws
materials.note: About 10 screws needed if not using a brad nailer.

cutList.material: 1x board, in your desired depth (1x8, 1x10, or 1x12)
cutList.note: 10" tall example — cut to about 1" less than your cabinet opening height.

cutList.material: 1x board, in your desired depth (1x8, 1x10, or 1x12)
cutList.note: 24" wide example — cut 2" narrower than your desired final width.

cutList.material: 1x8 board or 1/4" plywood, for the back

steps.title: Cut the side boards
steps.body: Cut two boards to match the desired height of your under-sink organizer. The overall height should be about 1" less than the cabinet opening so the organizer can be placed under the sink easily.
materials: 1x board, in your desired depth (1x8, 1x10, or 1x12)

steps.title: Attach the shelf board
steps.body: Cut the shelf board 2" narrower than your desired final width, giving about 1/2" of clearance to fit under the sink. Attach with screws or 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. If using a pass-through back design, position the shelf so a 1x8 board can fit above it for the back.
materials: 1x board, in your desired depth (1x8, 1x10, or 1x12)
1-1/4" brad nails or 1-1/2" SPAX self-tapping screws

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: If using plywood, cut a piece of 1/4" plywood to fit either the upper part of the back (pass-through) or the full back. Attach with brad nails or screws and glue.
materials: 1x8 board or 1/4" plywood, for the back
1-1/4" brad nails or 1-1/2" SPAX self-tapping screws

images.alt: A simple wood shelf organizer built to fit under a kitchen sink
